title: Multiprocessor Communication: Buses and Interconnection Networks
summary: Compares bus-based and network-based communication in multiprocessor systems, covering trade-offs in simplicity, performance, and scalability.
content_points: Bus: economic, simple, lower performance; does not scale; cache coherence is critical.
Interconnection network: flexible, higher performance; supports more parallel accesses.
Total connection: every processor directly connected to all others.
Partial connection: some processor pairs connected indirectly.
learning_objectives: Compare bus and network-based multiprocessor communication
Explain why buses do not scale well
